[[File:Kxakxa map.svg|thumb|Map of the homeland]]
[[File:Ringworld map.png|thumb|The ringworld as seen from the ground]]

Notes for the delta-dwelling culture whose language seeds the Ringworld setting. Aelaki itself is a descendant / later stratum; this page covers the '''original culture''' — its language names, sound system, grammar, numerals, writing history, and sample phrases.

== Language names ==

* '''Kxaki''' — "delta language". The everyday speech.
* '''Kxakxa''' — "delta-of-deltas". The prestige variety, spoken in the city of the same name (the setting's equivalent of Rome).
* '''Maki''' — "mother language" / "river language". The older form from which Kxaki descends.

See also: [[Ringworld basic syntax words]], [[Ringworld declensions]], [[Ringworld Gaiad]], [[Aelateun religion]].

== Phonology ==

=== Vowels ===

Eight emic vowels, arranged on a front/back × close/mid/open grid.

{| class="wikitable"
|+ Emic vowel inventory
!
! front
! back
|-
! close
| i
| u
|-
! near-close
| ɪ
| ʊ
|-
! close-mid
| e
| o
|-
! open
| æ
| ʌ~ɑ~ɔ (romanised ''a'')
|}

A ninth vowel '''ÿ''' /y/ (close front rounded) appears in some roots.

Diphthongs do not exist: two adjacent vowels are always split by a glottal stop. '''ʔ''' is treated as the absence of an onset rather than a phoneme in its own right.

'''w''' and '''y''' are allophones of a single approximant. The realisation is chosen to contrast with the two adjacent vowels ("inverse colouring"): ''iwi'' vs. ''oyo''. At syllable codas the approximant forms diphthong-like glides.

=== Consonants ===

38 consonants, organised across three places of articulation (bilabial, alveolar, velar) plus the glottal stop.

{| class="wikitable"
|+ Consonant inventory
!
! bilabial
! alveolar
! velar
! glottal
|-
! plosive (voiceless / voiced / implosive)
| p / b / ɓ
| t / d / ɗ
| k / g / ɠ
| ʔ
|-
! nasal
| m
| n
| ŋ
|
|-
! affricate (voiceless / voiced)
| pɸ / bβ
| t̠ʃ / d̠ʒ
| kx / ɡɣ
|
|-
! fricative (voiceless / voiced)
| f / v
| s~ʃ / z~ʒ
| x~h / ɣ
|
|-
! click (voiceless / voiced / nasalised)
| pʘ / bʘ / mʘ
| tǃ / dǃ / nǃ
| kǁ / ɡǁ / ŋǁ
|
|-
! approximant
| w
| r, l
|
|
|}

With 8 vowels and an empty-onset slot, the syllabary gives roughly '''304 attested syllabics'''.

== Grammar ==

* '''Alignment''': tripartite — subject, object, and intransitive are each marked distinctly.
* '''Morphology''': agglutinative.

=== Nouns ===

* '''Singular''' is unmarked.
* '''Exhaustive plural''' — suffix ''-l''. Indicates ''all'' members of some arbitrary group ("all the X").
* '''Non-exhaustive plural''' — reduplication. Indicates ''some'' members, not necessarily all.

Full paradigms are on [[Ringworld declensions]].

=== Verbs ===

* '''Intransitive plurality agreement''' — verbs agree with subject plurality only when intransitive.
* '''Telicity''' is marked.
* '''TAM''': tense (past / present / future), aspect (perfective / imperfective / habitual), mood (realis / possibility / necessity).
* '''Evidentiality''' and '''mirativity''' are marked as speaker reactions, distinct from tense/aspect/mood.

== Numerals ==

The system is '''base-12''', built around the rings (''wiv'') visible in the sky.

{| class="wikitable"
|+ Cardinal numbers
! #
! Word
! Note
|-
| 1 || pam ||
|-
| 2 || bel ||
|-
| 3 || ɓad̠ʒ ||
|-
| 4 || mɪ ||
|-
| 5 || pfym ||
|-
| 6 || bvæ ||
|-
| 7 || fÿnǃ ||
|-
| 8 || vup ||
|-
| 9 || p'ib ||
|-
| 10 || b'el || also "two hands" (hand-dual form)
|-
| 11 || m'akx || "extra"
|-
| 12 || wiv || "rings"
|}

A separate set of glyphs exists as an '''alternative financial numeral system''': same pronunciation as the everyday tallies but different characters, deliberately harder to alter one digit into another.

[[File:Number system with alternative glyphs.png|thumb|Everyday tallies alongside the financial glyphs]]

== Writing system ==

Four historical stages, each growing out of the one before it:

# '''Tomb text''' — the earliest form, found in ancient tombs. Almost entirely logographic, with heavy use of rebus.
# '''Temple text''' — phono-semantic compounds emerge and replace most rebus writing; glyph shapes are simplified.
# '''Diplomatic text''' — common characters are simplified into a kana-like phonetic script.
#* ''3.5 Late diplomatic text'': kana are absorbed back into new phono-semantic compounds (modelled on the historical Khitan script).
# '''Mercantile text''' — kana are replaced by a hangul-like syllable block system; older kana survive only inside legacy characters.
#* ''4.5 Late mercantile text'': newer characters have the hangul-like structure built in from the start.

The number '''12''' / ''wiv'' is written as a pictogram of two arches — interpreted as "the house of houses", with a single-arch "house" character as its base.

=== Pictograms ===
<gallery mode="packed" heights="120">
File:Rings character.png|Tomb-script rings character (''wiv'', also the numeral 12)
File:World house 12.png|"House of houses" — the 12 / rings pictogram
File:Delta pictoram.png|Delta (''Kxa'') pictogram
File:Tallies 1-6.png|Numbers 1–6
File:Regional variants in tallies.png|Regional variants in the tally system; the bottom two are unlucky asymmetrical glyphs
File:Time and minight tomb script.png|Tomb-script midnight character
File:Tomb script time character.png|Tomb-script time character — distinguished from midnight by the shadow acting as an arrow
File:Noon representation.png|Noon / day (''ɓÿw'')
File:Sunrise.png|Sunset (facing north; shadow rises in the east)
File:Sunset.png|Sunrise (facing north; shadow sets in the west)
File:Summer.png|Summer
File:Broken rings.png|The broken rings of winter
File:Sun behind the rings.png|The sun behind the rings, a winter motif
File:Ki character development.png|Development of the ''Ki'' character from its original "language" meaning into kana
File:Kxaki.png|''Kxaki'' written in characters
</gallery>

== Sample phrases (stream of consciousness) ==

* '''bakx''' — interjection, roughly "what?"
* '''Zallax Kxa!u''' — "Go to the delta/marsh!" (''Zal-'' go, ''-lax'' imperative, ''Kxa'' delta/marsh, ''!u'' allative marker).
* '''ɓoɓox''' — reduplicated imperative of ''ɓo'' "go"; emphatic, "go do that thing, do it!"
* '''Killax !ÿ'conlang'!u''' — "Speak in your conlang." (''!ÿ'' "you" → glyph ꔌ.)
* '''Zunn!a''' — "Me?"
* '''Zɪn'la''' — "I see." (''zɪn'' see, ''-la'' declarative.)
* '''ʘawiv zɪnna zunɓi''' — "I see the great rings in the sky." A fixed phrase for religious awe at the ringworld itself (''ʘawiv'' "great twelve"; ''-na'' conjugates the verb for awe; ''-ɓi'' agentive). [[File:I see the rings.png|none|thumb|The phrase in script]]
* '''ŋornǃÿ''' — the name of the planet.
* '''Hippo''' — a kind of fish.

== Miscellaneous lexicon ==

* '''ɓÿw'wiv''' — "day-ring", i.e. clock; captures the sun's passage around the sky against the visible rings.

[[Category:Ringworld]]
[[Category:Git synced pages]]
